params.name: Dharma Statue
params.description:
On Nov 23, 532 AD, Dharma sailed from India to China to promote the Zen of Mahayana Buddhism. 

The 18-cm high Dharma statue is an artwork from Dehua kiln in the early Qing dynasty to commemorate Bodhidharma. Sitting down cross-legged on the ground, this statue has a gourd-shaped bald head, with thick eyebrows and big eyes. His front teeth are exposed through an open mouth. A yellow cassock draped over the body gives the monk an eminent appearance. It is one of the most previous treasures of the …
